title: Patta Chitta Online 2025 - E-Services of Tamil Nadu Land Records
intro: Patta Chitta Online 2025 allows citizens of Tamil Nadu to view, verify, transfer, and download land records online. This guide explains everything from Patta Chitta meaning to FMB, A-Register Extract, application status, and land record verification.

sections.content: Patta, also known as Record of Rights (RoR), is a legal document issued by the Tamil Nadu government in the name of the actual property owner. Patta Chitta includes details like Patta number, district, taluk, village, owner name, survey number, land type, land area, and tax details.
Chitta is a land revenue document maintained by the Village Administrative Officer (VAO) and Taluk office. It records land classification such as wetland (Nanjai) or dryland (Punjai).
Meaning of Patta Chitta
Patta Chitta, also known as Patta Chittam, is a legal certificate that verifies land ownership in Tamil Nadu. It records land details and income-related information of landholders.
Difference Between Patta and Chitta
Patta

items: Contains land area, owner name, location and survey details
Ensures property purchase from the rightful owner
Required for safe and legal property transactions

sections.content: Chitta

items: Maintained by VAO and Taluk office
Contains land survey number and owner details

sections.content: How to Apply for Patta Chitta Online in Tamil Nadu

items: Visit eservices.tn.gov.in and click View Patta Chitta
Enter survey number or patta number
Provide mobile number and request OTP
Download and print Patta Chitta certificate

sections.content: How to Verify TN Patta Chitta Online

items: Enter Application ID and authentication value
Submit captcha to view Patta Chitta status

sections.content: How to View Patta Chitta FMB Online
FMB (Field Measurement Book) records land measurements. The online FMB facility has replaced the earlier manual process.

items: Enter taluk, village, survey number and subdivision

sections.content: How to Check Poramboke Land Status

items: Enter district, taluk, village and survey details

sections.content: How to View A-Register Extract Online

items: Enter district, taluk, village and survey details
Verify via OTP to view A-Register Extract

sections.content: How to View Patta Order Copy (Rural)

items: Enter Application ID and authentication value

sections.content: How to View FMB Sketch Rural

items: Visit Tamil Nadu Land Records portal
Enter land details and authentication value

sections.content: How to View TSLR Extract Online

items: Visit Tamil Nadu Land Records portal
Enter district, ward, block and survey details

sections.content: Details Found in Patta Chitta Online Document
Documents Required for TN Patta Chitta

items: Electricity bill or previous tax receipt

sections.content: How to Transfer Patta in Tamil Nadu
Government Excluded Land Details

items: Enter land details and authentication value

sections.content: How to Change Name in Patta Chitta
Name change in Patta Chitta is not available online. Applicants must visit the Taluk or VAO office. Processing time is usually 15–30 days.
Districts Where Patta Chitta Is Available
Patta Chitta Mobile Applications
TamilNilam (Official App)
TamilNilam is a government app launched by NIC to access Patta Chitta land records using district, taluk, village and survey number.
TN Patta Chitta (Third-Party App)
TN Patta Chitta is a third-party app. Users should avoid sharing confidential information.
Impact of Patta Chitta on Transactions and Loans

items: Must be updated after sale or inheritance

sections.content: Contact Details for Tamil Nadu Land Records
Commissionerate of Survey and Settlement, No.1 Survey House, Kamarajar Salai, Chepauk, Chennai-600005 | Phone: 044-28591662

Patta Chitta is a vital land record document in Tamil Nadu that ensures legal ownership, transparency and smooth property transactions. Using online services makes land record access faster and more reliable.
